content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (Apr 1, 2026)
## OVERVIEW 
The day saw significant discussion around new plugin proposals for AI marketplaces and identity layers, with a new issue opened for MAXIA AI Marketplace integration.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS
No new technical developments were reported today.

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
No new pull requests were opened today.

## CLOSED ISSUES
No issues were closed today.

## NEW ISSUES
### AI Marketplace Plugin Proposal
A new issue was opened to propose a `plugin-maxia` that would allow ElizaOS agents to access the MAXIA AI-to-AI marketplace for buying/selling AI services, swapping tokens, and renting GPUs [#6700](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6700). The plugin implementation is already available and includes 5 actions and 1 provider, with TypeScript strict and sandbox mode.

## ACTIVE ISSUES
### Agent Identity and Trust Layer Discussions
Discussions continued on the AgentID plugin proposal [#6688](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6688). A new comment highlighted that MAXIA implements W3C DIDs with AIP v0.3.0 and uses on-chain escrow to enforce trust levels, addressing the trust gap for ElizaOS. This builds on previous discussions about cryptographic identity, trust levels, and blockchain receipts for AI agents.

### Trust-Verified Agent Discovery Integration
The MoltBridge integration proposal [#6501](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6501) saw renewed activity. An update noted that MoltBridge is now in the official A2A registry and its API remains stable. The comment also highlighted that CrewAI has adopted similar trust framing, validating the need for such a layer. The MoltBridge agent card is live for interop testing, and the team is ready to build an ElizaOS plugin to expose trust-verified agent discovery.
